title: Password Spraying Attack
story: SIEM correlation rule triggered: multiple accounts experiencing authentication
failures with low per-account frequency — a classic indicator of password spraying.
Unlike brute force attacks, password spraying attempts ONE or TWO common passwords
against MANY accounts to avoid account lockout policies.
Investigate the authentication logs and identify the attack pattern, source, and
whether any accounts were successfully compromised.

objectives.label: Count number of distinct accounts targeted
Identify the password used in the spray (common weak password)

hints: Password spraying produces low counts per user but high distinct user counts from one IP
Use: ... | stats dc(user) as unique_users, count by src_ip | where unique_users > 20
Look for the pattern: many users, 1-2 failures each, from same source
Check for any 'Accepted' events from the same attacker IP after the spray
splunk_queries: index=hex_lab sourcetype=hex_case4_* "Failed password" | rex field=_raw "for (?<user>\w+) from (?<src_ip>[\d.]+)" | stats dc(user) as targets, count by src_ip | sort -targets
